title: Power Grid Management
activity_flow: START: Logic.update() called each frame
GUARD: state.isGame() && !state.isPaused() → proceed
Logic calls Groups.update() → triggers all entity updates including PowerGraphUpdater
PowerGraphUpdater.update() → calls PowerGraph.update()
--- PowerGraph.update() ---
GUARD: if any consumer is cheating() → set all consumer power.status=1f, skip normal flow
STEP: getPowerNeeded() — iterate consumers[], sum requestedPower(consumer)*delta() for each shouldConsumePower==true
STEP: getPowerProduced() — iterate producers[], sum getPowerProduction()*delta()
STEP: record lastPowerNeeded, lastPowerProduced, lastScaledPowerIn/Out, lastCapacity, lastPowerStored
STEP: powerBalance.add((produced - needed + energyDelta) / Time.delta) — rolling 60-sample mean
STEP: energyDelta = 0
GUARD: all three lists empty → skip distribution
BRANCH: powerNeeded > powerProduced → useBatteries(deficit) — drain batteries proportionally, add to powerProduced
BRANCH: powerProduced > powerNeeded → chargeBatteries(excess) — fill batteries proportionally, charged=true
BRANCH: powerNeeded == powerProduced → no battery action
STEP: distributePower(needed, produced, charged) — compute coverage = min(1, produced/needed)
FOR each consumer: if buffered → increment power.status by coverage*requestedPower/capacity
FOR each consumer: if not buffered && shouldConsumePower → power.status = coverage
FOR each consumer: if not buffered && !shouldConsumePower → power.status = estimate
--- PowerNode link/connectivity update ---
EVENT: node placed → PowerNodeBuild.placed() → getPotentialLinks() BFS in radius → configureAny(other.pos())
EVENT: link toggled (Integer config) → if linked: unlink, PowerGraph.reflow(this) + reflow(other) → creates 2 new graphs
EVENT: link added → power.graph.addGraph(other.power.graph) — merges smaller graph into larger
--- PowerGraph.reflow(tile) ---
BFS from tile: queue.add(tile), while queue not empty: pop child, add(child), iterate child.getPowerConnections()

PowerGraph.add(build): assign build.power.graph=this; categorise: outputsPower&&consumesPower&&!buffered→producers+consumers; outputsPower&&consumesPower&&buffered→batteries; outputsPower only→producers; consumesPower only→consumers
--- PowerGraph.remove(tile) ---
FOR each connection of removed tile: create new PowerGraph, BFS from that neighbour (skip removed tile), graph.update() to clear power on isolated nodes
Old graph entity removed
--- NuclearReactor overload (node overload handling) ---
NuclearReactorBuild.updateTile() each tick:
STEP: productionEfficiency = fuel/itemCapacity
STEP: if fuel>0 && enabled: heat += fullness * heating * min(delta,4)
STEP: if timer(timerFuel, itemDuration/timeScale): consume() one fuel item
STEP: if no fuel: heat -= Time.delta/ambientCooldownTime
STEP: if heat>0: drain coolant liquid, heat -= liquidUsed * coolantPower
STEP: if heat > smokeThreshold: emit smoke particles
STEP: heat = clamp(heat, 0, 1)
BRANCH: heat >= 0.999 → OVERLOAD: Events.fire(Trigger.thoriumReactorOverheat), kill()
kill() → onDestroyed() → createExplosion()
createExplosion(): if shouldExplode() [warmup>=explosionMinWarmup && fuel>=5||heat>=0.5]: Damage.damage(radius*tilesize=19, dmg=5000), Fx.reactorExplosion, Puddles.deposit liquid, Effect.shake
--- GeneratorBuild.getPowerProduction() ---
returns: enabled ? powerProduction * productionEfficiency : 0f
--- GameStats tracking (indirect) ---
Logic event BlockBuildEndEvent: state.stats.buildingsBuilt++ or buildingsDeconstructed++
Logic event BlockDestroyEvent: state.stats.buildingsDestroyed++ (own team) or destroyedBlockCount.increment(block) (enemy)
NOTE: GameStats has no direct power metrics; power data lives in PowerGraph (lastPowerNeeded, lastPowerProduced, lastPowerStored, powerBalance)
key_methods: Logic.update(): main game loop, calls Groups.update()
PowerGraph.update(): computes powerNeeded/Produced, battery balance, calls distributePower()
PowerGraph.useBatteries(needed): drain all enabled batteries proportionally
PowerGraph.chargeBatteries(excess): charge all enabled batteries proportionally
PowerGraph.distributePower(needed, produced, charged): set power.status coverage ratio per consumer
PowerGraph.reflow(tile): BFS to rebuild full graph from tile
PowerGraph.add(build): add building to graph, classify producer/consumer/battery
PowerGraph.remove(tile): split graph into sub-graphs after node removal
PowerGraph.addGraph(graph): merge two graphs (smaller into larger)
PowerGraph.transferPower(amount): diode-driven transfer, charge or drain batteries, track energyDelta
PowerNode.PowerNodeBuild.placed(): auto-link to nearby nodes via getPotentialLinks()
PowerNode.getPotentialLinks(tile,team,callback): spatial BFS for valid link candidates
NuclearReactorBuild.updateTile(): heat management, fuel consumption, overload check → kill()
key_relationships: Logic orchestrates game loop → Groups.update() → PowerGraphUpdater → PowerGraph.update()
PowerGraph contains Seq<Building> producers, consumers, batteries, all
PowerGraph created by PowerGraph() constructor, entity=PowerGraphUpdater.create()
PowerNode.PowerNodeBuild config(Integer) → triggers PowerGraph.reflow() or addGraph()
PowerGraph.distributePower() sets Building.power.status (coverage ratio 0..1) used by all consumers
GameStats updated by Logic event listeners (BlockBuildEnd, BlockDestroy, UnitDestroy, etc.) — no power fields
branching_conditions: cheating mode: skip normal power calc, set status=1
buffered consumer: incremental status fill vs. instantaneous coverage assignment
shouldConsumePower true/false: full coverage vs. estimated coverage
fuel == 0: ambient cooldown instead of heating
link already present: unlink + reflow; link absent + valid: link + addGraph
important_notes: PowerGraph is updated via PowerGraphUpdater entity (arc Entity), added to Groups automatically
PowerGraph.update() runs every tick; no separate 'grid tick' rate
energyDelta tracks cross-graph diode transfers within one tick before being zeroed
powerBalance is a 60-sample WindowedMean used for UI display (getPowerBalance())
ImpactReactor likely has similar overload (not read, but extends PowerGenerator)
Battery charging/discharging is proportional across all batteries in graph
Graph merging: smaller.all.size graph merges into larger; old entity removed
Graph splitting on removal: each neighbour gets its own new BFS-built graph
GameStats has no power fields; power stats are read-only from PowerGraph getters
